title: The Instrument VST Review By Phil Speiser

transcript: [Music] what's going on y'all welcome back it's us let's get productive so today i want to talk about a new vst by phil spicer that's aptly titled the instrument now the instrument is a romper with some really expansive capabilities i mean just in a little bit of time that i've been using this thing it's become very clear to me that if i wanted to i could use this one instrument to not only create an entire track but i could create tracks easier and do things creatively that i might not have attempted had i not had this instrument and a lot of the reasons for that is because of its built-in core generator and rhythm and melody generator that on top of all of the dope sounds in this thing it really makes for a great experience so let's not waste any more time let's get right into it phil spizer the instrument let's do it so as always i have logic cued up and this is the instrument by phil spizer i gotta say right off the back i'm really pleased with the ui and how things look now the instrument comes loaded with over 100 uniquely crafted sounds it has a built-in cord and melodic rhythm generator it even has an algorithm inside of it that'll place things in correct scales for you it has a midi export it has a built-in shaper effect that'll kind of give you that side chain effect on your instruments and it's just a really cool tool that really aids you in the production process but it also doesn't skim in the sound design department so that's really cool because a lot of times when vsts specialize in a certain area like chord generators or things of that nature they'll skim on the sounds or they'll give you half-assed sounds but a really dope core generator or they'll give you really dope sounds but a really half-ass core generator this thing kind of does all of those things really well so let's dive in and look a little bit closer at what's happening in the ui here now very simple ui very simple tone very simple colors but the resolution is really crisp and really clean and everything about this thing is really bold just because of how clean the resolution is and how clean everything is placed on the ui so it's easy on the eye easy to see what's happening it's not flooded with effects and it's not flooded with knobs and faders and things like that but what it does give you is super useful and i'm gonna i'm gonna show you in a second just how useful some of these things are but like i said in the intro you can create an entire beat using this thing and not only create an entire beat but really take your beats to another level and do things creatively that you might not have done had you not had this instrument now if you look over here at the top you have your classic adsr section right over here over here to the side is where you're gonna have your list of different sound presets so phil spiser really went out of his way to create some really cool really dynamic and really different sounds if you click right over here it's going to take you out of your preset section and bring you over to some of your effects so you have a reverb section right here you have a delay section right over here you have your cut off knob your drown knob and your stab knob and you can play around with these things to really change the characteristics of your sound so as you can see just playing around with the cut off the drowning the staff it can already change the characteristics of your sound just by using that one section alone and that's not even getting into the reverb and the delays or the shaper which is really cool i'm going to show you that in a second you have your low cut your gain your pan knob and an air knob right here like i said you have your reverb in your delay right over here you have your shaper section and this is where you're going to be able to add some side chain effects to your sound so if you turn this thing on it'll take a sound that sounds like this and you'll notice when i turn it on and i can change the timing of the ducts right here so that effect right there the shaper effect is really cool you can also hit these arrows right over here and it'll take you to your filter effect and that's a whole nother effect so you have a filter right here where you can adjust the attack you can adjust the intensity of of the filter the timing and the shape so you can do one shot loop one loop two you can make those adjustments all in this shape or section so you can really shape the characteristics of your sound and really take your sounds to a whole nother level if you look down here at the bottom this is where things get really interesting this is one of my favorite parts about the vst i mean there's really cool sounds inside of this thing but the thing that really makes this stand out amongst other romplers is the fact that it has this core generator but it also has a rhythmic generator as well so you could take a sound that sounds like this hit this button until you get the chords and now you're playing chords and you can go through all of the different types of chords so you have everything from basic chords to neo soul r b you can go to neo so r b and chromatic 2 and then you can just play them you can hit this button right here where it says strum and it'll strum those chords for you super cool super cool stuff and you can change the key so you can go to d you can go to e you can go to d sharp so no matter what chords you want to play you can make all kinds of different progressions with that chord via the chord generator now things start to get really interesting in the algorithmic side of this thing and just the intelligence inside of this thing if you're using this vst on multiple tracks the vst will recognize the key that the other chords are being played in from the other tracks so say for instance your last track you played chords and you played the chords in d sharp minor when you open up the new instance of this vst and you put it into melody mode it's going to recognize that the last track was played in minor so it's going to automatically switch this thing into d sharp minor so that you're already locked into the key that you want to be playing in no matter what key you're hitting on the keyboard and what sound is input into this vst the sound that comes out of this thing is going to be whatever you have selected down here so melody mode automatically adjusts any key that you're playing on the keyboard to work in the scale that you're trying to have it work in inside of your workflow and that's awesome i mean at that point it's almost it's almost like what are we even doing like you really don't even you can't even make a mistake it's all creativity it's all just straight flow straight wave of creativity and i already know what a lot of you guys are saying that are watching this video a lot of the best things about music comes from the mistakes you know being able to search and figure out your sound and figure out what it is exactly that you're trying to do that's where you get some of the best sound and some of your best things um in music is through the mistakes but after you've gotten really creative and you've laid down the structure of your beat a lot of times you're trying to complete the beat and you don't want to make mistakes anymore you're not trying to feel your way around and jumble things
